rationale: The cited paper is used to support a core theoretical premise in the citing paper: satisfaction depends on whether experience meets or falls short of prior expectations. It appears in the theory development section and again in the discussion as part of the expectation-disconfirmation account that the citing paper adopts for its own price-based reference-point model. This is more than a passing background mention because it helps justify the mechanism behind the citing paper's hypotheses and interpretation of results.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Diehl and Poynor (2010) as prior evidence that expectation disconfirmation drives post-consumption satisfaction. It uses that logic as conceptual grounding for its own reference-dependent model in which price sets expectations and disconfirmation shapes subjective quality evaluations.
